description: Appraise alternative-investment performance accounting for illiquidity, stale pricing, non-normal return distributions, benchmark selection, and comparability with traditional assets

performance_challenges.illiquidity: Market prices unavailable; NAV relies on appraisals
performance_challenges.survivorship_bias: Failed funds drop out of indices inflating averages

comparability_traditional.issue: Public indices priced daily; alts appraisal-based — direct comparison overstates alts Sharpe
comparability_traditional.adjustment: Unsmooth returns before comparing, or use PME techniques

pme_methods.kaplan_schoar: Sum of discounted distributions / sum of discounted contributions vs. public market
pme_methods.direct_alpha: IRR of PE cash flows minus public-index IRR of same flows

appropriate_metrics.irr: Money-weighted for private funds (GP controls timing)
appropriate_metrics.moic: Multiple on invested capital; supplements IRR
appropriate_metrics.twr: Time-weighted for hedge funds where investor controls flows

appraise_performance.description: Compute performance with illiquidity and bias adjustments

errors.message: fund_type must be private_equity, private_debt, real_estate, or hedge_fund

anti_patterns.rule: Do not compute Sharpe ratios on appraisal-based NAVs uncritically
anti_patterns.why: Smoothed returns inflate Sharpe by suppressing variance; unsmooth first or the metric misleads

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use TWR for private equity funds
anti_patterns.why: TWR neutralises cash-flow timing that the GP deliberately chose; IRR reflects actual investor experience

anti_patterns.rule: Do not benchmark PE against an arbitrary public index
anti_patterns.why: Benchmark must match sector, leverage, and vintage; otherwise alpha is noise
